prediction.reasoning: This is a hypothetical prediction based on a simplified financial model and should not be considered financial advice. Stock prices are highly volatile and unpredictable. The prediction utilizes a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, which assumes that the logarithm of the stock price follows a random walk with a drift. For this simulation, we assumed an annualized drift (average return) of 12% and an annualized volatility of 22%, based on typical historical large-cap stock performance. The point estimate represents the median of the projected log-normal price distribution after 20 trading days. The confidence intervals are calculated using the quantiles of this log-normal distribution, reflecting the uncertainty inherent in price forecasting over this period.
